question_text: Two electrical devices are connected to the same power supply. Device X has a power rating of 500 W and Device Y has a power rating of 100 W. Which device will transfer energy more quickly, and why?
correct_answer: Device X will transfer energy more quickly because it has a higher power rating (500 W compared to 100 W). Power is the rate of energy transfer, so a higher power rating means more joules of energy are transferred per second.
explanation: Power tells us how quickly energy is being transferred. Device X has a power rating of 500 W, which means it transfers 500 joules of energy every second. Device Y only transfers 100 joules per second. Therefore, Device X transfers energy 5 times faster than Device Y.
